title: Frost Growth
description: Feathery ice creeping across a cold surface. Dendritic crystals nucleate along a leaf's veins, a rock's edges, a strand of barbed wire, a chain-link fence or the corners of a windowpane, then branch and fork outward in fern-like fronds until the whole surface is furred with frost. Warm the glass (move the pointer) to melt a clearing that re-freezes. Surface, growth speed, branchiness, feathering, thaw and palette are live and mappable.
